picks.game: New York Knicks @ San Antonio Spurs
picks.rationale: The New York Knicks enter Game 1 of the NBA Finals on an impressive 10-game winning streak, showcasing exceptional form and momentum. They have also been a dominant force against the spread, covering in 9 of their last 10 games. With Mitchell Robinson expected to play and bolster their league-leading playoff rebounding, the Knicks are well-equipped to keep this opening game competitive against the Spurs.

picks.game: New York Knicks @ San Antonio Spurs
picks.rationale: Both the Knicks and Spurs have demonstrated a strong offensive rhythm recently, with New York averaging 123.8 PPG and San Antonio averaging 116.3 PPG over their last 10 games. The head-to-head history also points towards a higher-scoring affair, as their last five matchups averaged 232.4 total points, with three of those games going Over the total. The Knicks have gone Over in 6 of their last 10 games, and the Spurs in 8 of their last 10, further supporting the Over.

picks.game: New York Knicks @ San Antonio Spurs
picks.rationale: The San Antonio Spurs hold a significant home-court advantage for Game 1 of the NBA Finals, a crucial factor in playoff series. They are coming off a hard-fought seven-game series victory in the Western Conference Finals, highlighting their resilience and ability to perform under pressure. Led by Victor Wembanyama, who is a strong contender for Finals MVP, the Spurs are the favorites to secure the opening win on their home floor.

picks.game: New York Knicks @ San Antonio Spurs
picks.rationale: While the Knicks have been excellent against the spread, the Spurs' home-court advantage in Game 1 of the Finals is a significant factor. San Antonio has won 6 of their last 10 games, including a two-game winning streak entering this series, demonstrating their ability to win convincingly. The impact of Victor Wembanyama at home could allow the Spurs to pull away and cover this spread, despite New York's strong ATS record.

picks.game: New York Knicks @ San Antonio Spurs
picks.rationale: Game 1 of the NBA Finals often features heightened defensive intensity and nerves, which can lead to a lower-scoring contest as teams adjust to the championship stage. Despite recent offensive trends, both teams may prioritize defense early in the series. The pressure of the Finals could result in a tighter, more deliberate pace, potentially keeping the combined score below the projected total of 217.5 points.

The Phillies hold a significant pitching advantage with Cristopher Sanchez (1.47 ERA, 44.2 scoreless innings) on the mound against the Padres' Walker Buehler (4.88 ERA). San Diego's offense has been anemic, going 0-27 with runners in scoring position over their last four games, and they were recently swept by the Phillies. Coupled with Philadelphia's recent dominance in head-to-head matchups, including winning the first two games of this series, the Phillies are well-positioned to cover the -1.5 run line at favorable odds.

The Milwaukee Brewers are in excellent form, boasting a 37-21 record and a strong 21-11 home record, while the Giants are struggling at 23-38 overall and 11-22 on the road. Milwaukee has dominated the first two games of this series, outscoring San Francisco 24-5. Giants' starter Logan Webb has been inconsistent with a 4.82 ERA, making it likely the high-powered Brewers offense will secure another multi-run victory.

The Texas Rangers are currently on a five-game winning streak and have already won the first two games of this road series against the Cardinals. A significant reverse line movement is evident, with the public heavily backing St. Louis (86-92% of bets), yet the moneyline has shifted towards Texas, indicating sharp money is on the Rangers. This strong situational trend and sharp money signal make the Rangers moneyline a high-confidence pick.

Both starting pitchers, Jeffrey Springs (4.07 ERA) for the Athletics and Colin Rea (4.70 ERA) for the Cubs, have ERAs above 4.00, suggesting potential for runs. The total line movement is a key indicator: the line opened at 9 with the under juiced, then dropped to 8.5, but the over subsequently gained significant juice, signaling sharp money expects more runs at the lower total. With both bullpens also reported as shorthanded, a higher-scoring game is anticipated.

The Los Angeles Angels possess a distinct pitching advantage with Walbert Urena (2.44 ERA) starting against the Colorado Rockies' Michael Lorenzen, who carries a struggling 7.22 ERA and 1.90 WHIP. Despite dropping the first two games of the series, the Angels are at home and are due for a bounce-back performance. The significant disparity in starting pitching quality provides a strong foundation for an Angels victory.
